headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Amazon.com (AMZN) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Share Price Run?
summary: Wondering if Amazon.com is still reasonably priced after its long run as a market favorite, or if the current share price already reflects most of the opportunity? The stock last closed at US$268.26, with returns of 1.6% over 7 days, 27.9% over 30 days, 18.4% year to date, 41.2% over 1 year and 153.9% over 3 years, compared to 63.0% over 5 years. Recent coverage has focused on Amazon.com's position in online retail and cloud services, along with its ongoing investments across logistics and...

headline: Amazon Lawsuit Puts Marketplace Safety And Long Term Costs In Focus
summary: Amazon.com (NasdaqGS:AMZN) is facing a new lawsuit in Washington over alleged failure to address safety concerns tied to a defective camp stove sold on its marketplace. The case follows a catastrophic injury involving a product labeled as a #1 Best Seller, challenging how Amazon manages consumer warnings for third party goods. The lawsuit focuses on Amazon’s product safety oversight, platform accountability, and duty of care to customers. For investors tracking Amazon.com at a share price...

headline: Data Center Expert Warns Gigawatt-Scale AI Buildouts Could Trigger Rolling Blackouts
summary: On a recent Prof G Markets segment, data center veteran John Perella described a near-miss in Virginia that should focus every infrastructure investor’s attention. “9 data centers went offline or they went to backup power. headline: GameStop offers to buy eBay for about $56 billion
summary: GameStop said on Sunday that it has proposed to buy all common shares of eBay Inc for about $56 billion.  CEO Ryan Cohen, GameStop's largest investor, said the offer price ‌is $125 a share in a 50-50 mix of cash and stock.  Based on eBay's Friday close, ‌the bid represents a premium of about 20%.

content.title: GameStop offers to buy eBay for about $56 billion
content.summary: GameStop said on Sunday that it has proposed to buy all common shares of eBay Inc for about $56 billion.  CEO Ryan Cohen, GameStop's largest investor, said the offer price ‌is $125 a share in a 50-50 mix of cash and stock.  Based on eBay's Friday close, ‌the bid represents a premium of about 20%.
